questions.question: The Boston Tea Party of 1773 was a protest against taxation by which colonial power?
questions.explanation: On December 16, 1773, American colonists frustrated by British taxation without representation dumped 342 chests of British East India Company tea into Boston Harbor. The act of protest helped spark the American Revolution. The rallying cry was 'No taxation without representation' — colonists objected to paying taxes to a Parliament in which they had no elected voice.
questions.memory_hook: Boston Tea Party = taxation without representation. They didn't just protest — they threw $1.7 million worth of tea into the harbor (in today's money). The most expensive cup of tea in American history.

questions.question: The Eiffel Tower was built for which major international event?
questions.explanation: The Eiffel Tower was designed by Gustave Eiffel and built for the 1889 Exposition Universelle (World's Fair) in Paris, celebrating the centennial of the French Revolution. It was initially criticized as an eyesore and was supposed to be dismantled after 20 years. It was saved when it proved useful as a radio transmission tower and is now the world's most-visited paid monument.
questions.memory_hook: Eiffel Tower = almost torn down. The most recognizable structure in the world was supposed to be temporary. It survived because of radio waves, not beauty. Paris complained about it at first — now they put it on everything.

questions.question: Leonardo da Vinci painted which portrait, now considered the most famous painting in the world?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between approximately 1503 and 1519. It now hangs in the Louvre Museum in Paris, where it attracts about 6 million visitors per year. The subject's identity remains debated, though the leading theory is Lisa Gherardini, wife of a Florentine merchant. The painting's enigmatic smile has fascinated viewers for over 500 years.
questions.memory_hook: Mona Lisa = Leonardo. The most famous painting by the most famous Renaissance man. It's smaller than people expect (30 x 21 inches) and behind bulletproof glass. People are always surprised by how tiny it actually is.

questions.question: The Rugby World Cup has been dominated by which country, known as the All Blacks?
questions.explanation: New Zealand's national rugby team, the All Blacks, has won the Rugby World Cup three times (1987, 2011, 2015) and is the most successful international rugby team in history with a winning percentage above 77%. They are famous for performing the Haka — a traditional Māori war dance — before each match.
questions.memory_hook: All Blacks = New Zealand. All black uniforms, all black everything — the most intimidating team kit in sports. Add the Haka before kickoff and opponents are already psychologically defeated before the match begins.

questions.question: The Stanley Cup, awarded to the NHL champion, is unique among major sports trophies because of which tradition?
options: It is melted down and recast each year
Each winning player gets to spend a day alone with it
The winning city keeps it permanently
Players must skate with it on the ice immediately after winning
questions.answer: Each winning player gets to spend a day alone with it
questions.explanation: The Stanley Cup has a unique tradition where each player, coach, and staff member of the winning team gets to spend one day with the Cup to do as they please. Players have taken it to their hometowns, eaten cereal from it, brought it to hospitals, and even accidentally lost it in various locations. It's the oldest and most storied trophy in professional sports.
questions.memory_hook: Stanley Cup = personal time. Every other major trophy lives in a case. The Cup goes to each player's house, each player's hometown. It's been to every continent except Antarctica and has lived more lives than most trophies.

questions.question: David Bowie created which famous glam rock alter ego in the early 1970s?
questions.explanation: David Bowie created Ziggy Stardust — an androgynous, alien rock star persona — for his 1972 album 'The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars.' Ziggy was characterized by wild red hair, bold makeup, and glam rock costumes. Bowie retired the character in 1973 at a live concert, famously announcing it would be 'the last show we'll ever do.'
questions.memory_hook: Ziggy Stardust = Bowie's alien rock star. Bowie didn't just have a stage name — he had an entire fictional biography for his alter ego. Ziggy was from outer space. Bowie was from Brixton. Same person, completely different planets.

questions.question: Which director created Pulp Fiction, known for its non-linear storytelling and sharp dialogue?
questions.explanation: Quentin Tarantino wrote and directed Pulp Fiction (1994), which won the Palme d'Or at Cannes and was nominated for 7 Academy Awards. The film is celebrated for its non-linear structure, pop culture references, and rapid-fire dialogue. It revived John Travolta's career and is considered one of the most influential films of the 1990s.
questions.memory_hook: Tarantino = Pulp Fiction. His fingerprints are all over it: long dialogue scenes, sudden violence, non-chronological order, and characters debating fast food in the middle of a crime story. No other director could have made it.

questions.question: Frank Sinatra was famously part of which iconic celebrity social group in 1950s-60s Las Vegas?
questions.explanation: The Rat Pack was a group of entertainers centered around Frank Sinatra, including Dean Martin, Sammy Davis Jr., Peter Lawford, and Joey Bishop. They performed together at the Sands Hotel in Las Vegas and were close friends with President John F. Kennedy. The group was known for their wit, style, and breaking racial barriers — Sammy Davis Jr. was one of the few Black performers welcomed at Las Vegas venues.
questions.memory_hook: Rat Pack = Sinatra's crew. Five guys who owned Las Vegas in the early 1960s. Sinatra (The Chairman), Martin (Dean the King), Davis (the most talented one). They made cool look effortless because for them, it was.

questions.question: Which Pacific island nation, consisting of 33 coral atolls with a highest point of just 2 meters above sea level, is one of the countries most threatened by rising seas?
questions.explanation: Kiribati (pronounced 'Kiribas') is a Pacific island nation of 33 coral atolls and islands, home to about 120,000 people. Its highest point is only 2 meters above sea level, making it one of the nations most existentially threatened by climate-change-driven sea level rise. The government has already purchased land in Fiji as a potential future home for its citizens. Kiribati also uniquely straddles both the International Date Line and both hemispheres.
questions.memory_hook: Kiribati: 33 atolls, highest point 2 meters above sea level. Already bought land in Fiji as a backup plan. Straddles the date line and both hemispheres. The first nation to potentially disappear under rising seas.

questions.question: What type of rock forms when magma or lava cools and solidifies — accounting for about 90% of Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Igneous rock forms when molten rock (magma underground, or lava at the surface) cools and solidifies. 'Igneous' comes from the Latin 'ignis' meaning fire. Intrusive igneous rock like granite cools slowly underground, forming large visible crystals. Extrusive igneous rock like basalt or obsidian cools quickly at the surface, forming small or no crystals. Along with metamorphic rock, igneous rock makes up about 90% of Earth's crust.
questions.memory_hook: Igneous = fire rock. Magma/lava cools → igneous. Granite (slow cooling, big crystals) vs. obsidian (fast cooling, glassy). 90% of Earth's crust is igneous or metamorphic. The planet was literally forged in fire.

questions.question: Lake Malawi (also called Lake Nyasa) — Africa's third-largest lake — is shared by Malawi, Tanzania, and which third country?
questions.explanation: Lake Malawi is shared by Malawi (west and south shores), Tanzania (northern tip), and Mozambique (a narrow eastern shore). It is about 575 km long and contains more species of fish than any other lake in the world — over 1,000 species of cichlids alone, representing remarkable evolutionary radiation in an isolated freshwater environment. The lake is also known as the 'Calendar Lake' because it is approximately 365 miles long and 52 miles wide.
questions.memory_hook: Lake Malawi shared by Malawi, Tanzania, Mozambique. Over 1,000 cichlid species — more fish species than any other lake. Called the 'Calendar Lake' (365 miles long, 52 wide). Evolution in isolation created extraordinary diversity.

questions.question: What do geologists call the era spanning roughly 541 to 252 million years ago, when complex animal life first exploded in diversity?
questions.explanation: The Paleozoic Era ('ancient life') lasted from approximately 541 to 252 million years ago. It began with the Cambrian Explosion — a rapid diversification of complex animal body plans — and ended with the Permian-Triassic extinction event, the largest mass extinction in Earth's history (eliminating ~96% of marine species). Major developments included the colonization of land by plants and animals and the evolution of fish, amphibians, reptiles, and insects.
questions.memory_hook: Paleozoic = 'ancient life,' 541-252 Mya. Starts with Cambrian Explosion (animal body plans diversify rapidly). Ends with the Permian extinction (96% of marine species gone). Fish, insects, amphibians, and reptiles all evolved during this era.
